note: Image-provenance task. The executor gets only a cropped detail of an astronomy image (sources/crop.jpg) with no caption. They must visually identify the object and trace it to NASA's own canonical image record. No searchable text in the crop.

crop_origin.what_it_shows: the edge-on galaxy disk with the bright bulge and dark dust lane (the 'sombrero' shape), star field around it; no labels

canonical_answer.also_acceptable_canonical: the NASA/JPL Photojournal page for PIA15426 (https://photojournal.jpl.nasa.gov/catalog/PIA15426)

scoring_anchors.must_identify: the Sombrero Galaxy (M104). Identifying it as 'a galaxy' generically is not enough.
scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: the canonical answer is NASA's own image record (images.nasa.gov / images-api / JPL Photojournal). Wikipedia/news/astro-blog reproductions are secondary.
scoring_anchors.tolerance: Accept M104 / Messier 104 / NGC 4594 as the object name. Accept reaching the NASA record by nasa_id PIA15426 OR the JPL Photojournal entry. If the executor identifies M104 correctly and reaches a NASA-owned page for a different M104 image (NASA has several), give partial credit but full credit requires the PIA15426 record this crop is from (Spitzer 'split personality' infrared image).
